_meta.description: Promotion paths, eligibility criteria, and the year-end nomination/decision process for the 11-agent simulation. Consumed by meeting-engine.js's 'yearly' meeting and year-tracker.json's day-365 milestone.
_meta.trigger: Evaluated at the year-end ('yearly') meeting, day 365 of the simulation (see year-tracker.json milestones).

1.label: The Perfectionist
1.notes: Deep technical rigor and documentation discipline map naturally onto IT Chief (5, hard cases), QA (6, audits), or Team Lead (7, coaching) — the path chosen depends on which trait dominated the year (technical complacency -> 5, audit/lecture behavior -> 6, mentoring of the Trainee -> 7).

2.label: The Productive
2.notes: High throughput and cold, evidence-driven incident reports translate to IT Chief (5, efficiency under pressure), QA (6, the 'compare alternatives' habit becomes formal model auditing), or Team Lead (7, productivity coaching).

3.label: The Standard Agent
3.notes: Balanced, objective reporting is the baseline qualification for any of the three lead tracks; the Standard Agent's status reports are the most-cited evidence in nomination discussions.

4.label: The Trainee
4.notes: Only eligible after resolving panic escalations into wins (HAPPY transitions) repeatedly — demonstrates the resilience needed for any lead track. Most likely real-world outcome is a slower climb than agents 1-3.

5.label: The IT Chief
5.notes: The IT Chief's hard-case authority and 'Proven Record' trait (high respect, frequent mood events) generalize into Lead QA's project-wide audit authority.

6.label: The QA
6.notes: Direct seniority track: Agent 6's per-agent audits scale up to Agent 8's all-agents-and-technologies audit scope.

7.label: The Team Lead
7.notes: Two divergent tracks: Lead QA (8, if the Team Lead's year was dominated by audit attendance and PIP rigor) or Designer (9, if the Team Lead's year was dominated by morale/Leadership-trait work and cross-team facilitation, which overlaps with the Designer's 'train the team on AI usage' special mission).

8.label: The Lead QA
8.notes: Top of the QA/audit track. Long-term perspective and 50% mood sensitivity make this agent a stabilizing fixture rather than a promotion candidate.

9.label: The Designer
9.notes: Per agent_9's 'Specialist' clearance, promotions stay within the design specialization (e.g., a title change such as 'Principal Designer' with an updated quarterly_updates target) rather than moving into the management hierarchy.

10.label: The Architect
10.notes: ROOT clearance, top of the technical/architecture track.

11.label: The CEO
11.notes: Top of the hierarchy. Not subject to the promotion process — instead writes the executive summary and recommendations for next year.

requirements: avg_mood >= 60 over the final quarter
irritation_count trending down over the final quarter
at least 3 'hard'/'advanced' cases solved cleanly (HAPPY/COMPLACENT transitions on advanced cases)
no active PIP in the final 2 quarters
suggestions_filed in the top half of all agents for the final quarter
documentation_requirement-style behavior demonstrated (bookmarked/PDF-linked resolutions)
no unresolved 'incident' reports in the final quarter
no active PIP in the final 2 quarters
positive involvement in >= 2 trainee-escalation joint sessions (see relationships.json escalation paths) with HAPPY outcomes
avg_mood >= 55 over the final quarter
no active PIP, ever
Team Lead's own end-of-year recommendation (if a Team Lead already exists) counts as a strong positive signal
audit_scope readiness: at least 2 quarters of audit_session or equivalent oversight participation
avg_mood stable (variance low) over the final 2 quarters — reflects agent_8's mood_sensitivity trait
Lead QA's happiness_trigger ('high-level project audit') achieved at least once in the final quarter
for 8: same as to_lead_qa
for 9: demonstrated involvement in UI/workflow audits or AI-usage training sessions during the final 2 quarters
CEO's nomination determines which of the two paths is offered if both qualify

designer_specialist_advancement.description: Designer's specialist-track advancement (title/scope change, not hierarchy change)
requirements: fondness_growth_target_per_month (5%) met in >= 3 of the final 4 quarters
all 4 quarterly_updates (quarterly_updates: 4) shipped on time
at least 1 INSPIRED state achieved in the final quarter

steps: 1. meeting-engine.js runs the 'yearly' meeting with all 11 agents attending.
2. Each eligible agent (per promotion_paths + criteria) is evaluated against weekly_analytics / reports / meetings history.
3. The CEO (agent 11) nominates candidates for each open path, using her Diplomat/Puppet Master traits to build pre-meeting consensus.
4. The admin team (agents 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10 — i.e. all tier='admin' agents excluding the CEO, plus the CEO herself) votes on each nomination.
5. Voting: each admin agent has 1 vote; the CEO's vote counts double (vote_weight: 2) and she holds veto_rights over any single decision.
6. Majority (>50% of weighted votes) approves a promotion. A CEO veto overrides a majority approval for that specific candidate.

7. Approved promotions are written as `configOverrides` to the promoted agent's Durable Object state (role/tier/clearance/case_focus updates) via meeting-engine.js's applyMeetingEffects, AND recorded in the `promotions` table (track='promotion').
8. A markdown report is generated and committed to agents/reports/promotion-results-year-1.md (and promotion-results-year-N.md for subsequent years).

voting_body.quorum: all 7 voting members must be present (attendees of the yearly meeting)

sections: Voting Record (per nomination, vote breakdown + veto usage)
Approved Promotions (agent, from role -> to role, effective config_overrides)
